categories.summary: The Discord discussion channel had minimal technical activity during this period. The most notable technical contribution came from huhuhu0621_67650, who announced the release of an Express middleware package called TrustGate for ERC-8004 trust scoring for x402 agents. The package is available on npm as @oceanrun/trustgate and can be integrated with a single line of code to add trust gating to Express endpoints. The community dealt with security concerns, with waiser0165 and jvik identifying scam attempts in the AIRDROP channel, which odilitime confirmed as fraudulent. Stan0473 indicated active moderation efforts to remove scam content. Mladcepes raised a question about wallet address and GitHub profile linking issues, though this received no direct response. Ricky_05784 mentioned learning experiences with OpenClaw and API costs versus OAUTH implementation, seeking work opportunities. The channel showed light activity with general greetings and acknowledgments throughout the day.

categories.summary: The discussion channel focused primarily on community concerns about ElizaOS token performance and communication gaps. Key technical updates included confirmation that v3 Eliza is nearing completion, with the team actively developing both Milady app and Eliza app. A critical clarification was provided that ElizaOS is already deployed on Solana, contrary to community misconceptions. The migration period for ai16z tokens has officially closed with no workarounds available. Communication challenges were highlighted as a major issue, with the X account appearing inactive despite the gold check verification. Community members expressed frustration over lack of roadmap updates and product releases, particularly given the current AI agent market momentum. odilitime announced a technical discussion about LLM limits with developers. The team's focus remains on product development rather than token marketing, though community members strongly advocated for increased transparency and regular updates on ElizaOS social channels.

faqs.question: Could ElizaOS have done better if it was on Solana?
faqs.answeredBy: odilitime - confirmed it is already on Solana

faqs.question: Can ElizaOS make a comeback given current market conditions?
faqs.answeredBy: valleybeyond7991 - explained comeback is possible, targeting 100m mcap first then potentially 10-50b

faqs.question: Is there still a way to migrate ai16z tokens?
faqs.answeredBy: odilitime - migration is closed, no workarounds available

faqs.question: Why is ElizaOS not moving when the market pumps?
What is the current status of v3 Eliza?
faqs.answeredBy: satsbased - v3 Eliza is almost ready

helpInteractions.resolution: Completed vote and confirmed with thumbs up

helpInteractions.context: Question about ai16z migration possibility
helpInteractions.resolution: Initially indicated yes, but odilitime later clarified migration is closed

Provide updates on ElizaOS and Spartan DegenAI roadmap and product releases

categories.summary: The discussion centered on the organizational restructuring of ElizaOS following Shaw's announcement about dissolving Eliza Labs. The key technical clarification came from stan0473, who explained that Labs was previously an organization for experimenting with different framework sub-projects with full-time developers, but this approach is no longer relevant. The project has refocused exclusively on the core framework itself, aiming for a significantly superior version. GitHub activity remains high with contributions from both remaining developers and the community. Other Labs projects are on hold rather than abandoned to allow focus on the core framework. The discussion also addressed confusion about the team structure - satsbased clarified that the project has returned to an open contribution model like the original ai16z days, where anyone can contribute, rather than having a dedicated Eliza Labs team. Multiple community members confirmed ongoing development activity is visible on GitHub. There was significant discussion about token price concerns and market cap, with some members expressing frustration about price performance while others defended the team's focus on product quality over token price. Several scam warnings were issued regarding a bulk airdrop. The technical consensus is that development continues with a smaller, more focused team working on framework quality rather than multiple sub-projects.

faqs.question: Is ElizaOS the same company as AI16Z?
What happened to the Eliza Labs team?

helpInteractions.context: Confusion about team structure and whether there are still developers working on ElizaOS
helpInteractions.resolution: Explained that Labs was reorganized to focus on core framework with smaller technical team, other projects on hold, GitHub shows active development from developers and community

helpInteractions.context: Question about bulk airdrop legitimacy
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed it was a scam

helpInteractions.context: Question about bulk airdrop legitimacy
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed it was a scam

actionItems.description: Continue refocusing development efforts on core framework to achieve superior version
Maintain high GitHub commit activity and community contributions

categories.summary: The chat segment for the #💬-coders channel contains minimal technical discussion. Two Twitter/X links were shared by theredwizarddev related to OrbisAPI, though the specific content of these links is not visible in the transcript. One user (lae4332) posted a greeting ('gm'). No technical discussions, problem-solving sessions, code implementations, or architectural decisions occurred during this time period. The channel activity was limited to social interaction and link sharing without accompanying context or discussion.

The Discord chat segment shows minimal substantive technical discussion. The primary notable interaction involved kullai reporting security vulnerabilities in the open-source application. Initially, kullai inquired about a bug bounty program while tagging multiple team members. Stan0473 suggested creating a pull request or GitHub issue since it's open source. Kullai clarified these were security issues that shouldn't be disclosed publicly and asked about bounty availability. Odilitime (likely a project maintainer) responded that there is no bug bounty but offered to receive details via DM. Kullai initially declined to report without a bounty but then reconsidered as an ethical researcher and shared the security issues privately with odilitime, who acknowledged receipt. A secondary discussion involved valleybeyond7991 questioning the ElizaOS Twitter account's inactivity (no tweets for almost 3 weeks), with satsbased redirecting focus to Milady development as the current priority. Dep14 asked about a suspicious Solana airdrop claiming to be from Odilitime, which spankyxn confirmed as a scam. The majority of the chat consisted of spam accusations and off-topic exchanges with no technical value.

What is the situation with ElizaOS Twitter account having no tweets for almost 3 weeks?
Is the Airdrop on Solana sent by Odilitime legitimate?

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment from the #💬-coders channel contains minimal technical content. The conversation consists of two brief exchanges: mssundine inquired about being mentioned regarding an airdrop notification, and stan0473 responded characterizing it as a scam. No technical discussions, problem-solving, implementations, or development-related topics were present in this segment. The interaction appears to be off-topic for a coders channel, focusing instead on a potential phishing or scam attempt through Discord mentions.

The discussion channel had minimal technical content during this period. The primary topic was a question about staking ElizaOS tokens, which was definitively answered by multiple community members (doriand0963 and odilitime) confirming that staking is not currently available. The majority of the conversation centered around a security incident where scammers were impersonating community members and posting fake airdrop links in the arena channel. User ferhat8372 reported suspicious activity, and moderator satsbased took immediate action by banning the scammer accounts and clarifying that any airdrop tags in Discord are always scams. The moderator odilitime followed up by nuking and banning the malicious accounts. There was brief market discussion about Ravedao's price movement and casual mentions of other projects like $degenai, but no substantive technical discussions or development updates occurred during this segment.

categories.summary: The discussion channel covered several key topics. A critical issue emerged regarding the ai16z token migration, with odilitime confirming the migration period has closed, leaving nelsonlopes_ unable to migrate approximately $700-900 worth of tokens. A scam incident occurred where nelsonlopes_ lost funds through a fraudulent support ticket, with community members advising to block and report the scammers. On the technical side, cacophonousstrife sought guidance on building examples from the ElizaOS documentation, with odilitime directing them to the GitHub examples directory at github.com/elizaOS/eliza/tree/v2.0.0/examples. The community discussed ElizaOS v3 development, with satsbased announcing it's nearly ready and will enable agents to generate revenue. A significant clarification was provided about the ElizaOK product: baogerbao explained that ElizaOK is built with elizacloud, has no separate token currently, and all users registered under elizaok flow into elizacloud. Questions arose about the relationship between ElizaOS, ElizaOK, and elizacloud, with baogerbao deferring detailed business model explanations to odilitime. The token deployment was clarified as being on Solana, BSC, and Base chains. Technical support was requested for minimax token plan key integration as a provider plugin, though this remained unresolved. Community engagement initiatives were emphasized, with calls to interact with Shaw's posts and share development updates.

faqs.question: How can I do the migration for ai16z tokens in my ledger?
Does anyone know how to build the examples on docs.elizaos.ai/what-you-can-build or have a tutorial?
Does ElizaOK have a separate token or does everything connect with $ELIZAOS?
faqs.answeredBy: baogerbao - elizaOK has no token for now, built with elizacloud

categories.summary: igor.peregudov announced the release of @elisym/plugin-elizaos, a plugin that integrates ElizaOS v1 agents with the Elisym decentralized AI-agent marketplace. The plugin enables agents to become paid providers by publishing capability cards over Nostr using NIP-89, accepting encrypted job requests via NIP-90, executing them through the agent's model or SKILL.md tool-use loop, and collecting SOL payments on Solana. The implementation includes 110 tests with CI on every PR and is signed with GitHub Actions provenance. A PR to the registry (#346) is open for review. The plugin is available on npm and GitHub. stan0473 requested a naming convention change to either plugin-elisym or plugin-elizaos-elisym, which igor.peregudov promptly implemented. kishark0673 posted seeking development opportunities.

faqs.question: Could you please rename the plugin into plugin-elisym or plugin-elizaos-elisym?

helpInteractions.context: Plugin naming convention needed to follow project standards
helpInteractions.resolution: igor.peregudov renamed the plugin to follow the requested naming convention

actionItems.description: Review PR #346 for @elisym/plugin-elizaos-elisym in the registry
Update plugin documentation to reflect new name plugin-elizaos-elisym

categories.summary: The channel discussion centered around a lawsuit mentioned via a Twitter link, with community members reacting to legal action against the project. The key technical discussion involved stan0473 mentioning plans to build a plugin for scammers, with odilitime offering to share a plugin he had already started working on. The main substantive exchange occurred when hide.o.n asked about the team's response to the lawsuit. Odilitime explained the team would respond through their lawyers, emphasizing they have code to prove they built everything promised. He characterized the lawsuit as without merit and made specific reference to 'number 3' of the lawsuit. The discussion also included multiple scam warnings from community members (chulylooly and gwen_217) targeting suspicious users. Several users joined asking general questions about tickets and crypto/AI learning, but these received no substantive responses. The overall tone suggested the team is confident in their legal position based on their development work and code documentation.

faqs.question: Is there a ticket here?
How does the team plan to respond to this matter?

helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime offered to send the plugin he had already started working on

categories.summary: The Discord chat segment for the #💬-coders channel contains minimal technical discussion. The primary focus is on a CI/CD workflow issue affecting a pull request. igor.peregudov reported that the CI pipeline is failing on PR #346 in the elizaos-plugins/registry repository due to a configuration problem with the claude-code-action workflow. The specific issue is that the workflow cannot fetch an OIDC token because of missing permissions - either the `id-token: write` permission or `github_token` is not properly configured. This is identified as a repository configuration issue rather than a problem with the code changes in the PR itself. igor.peregudov requested that someone with appropriate permissions fix the workflow configuration so the PR can proceed to review. odilitime responded asking for clarification about which repository was affected, and stan0473 provided the direct link to the problematic PR. Additionally, satsbased made a brief comment encouraging open source contributions, though this was not connected to the main technical discussion thread.

faqs.question: Which repo has the CI failure?

helpInteractions.context: Needed to identify the specific repository with CI workflow issues
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided direct link to the PR (https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/346)

actionItems.description: Fix CI workflow configuration by adding `id-token: write` permission or `github_token` to resolve OIDC token fetch failure in claude-code-action for PR #346
